title: Oligoneuron × lutescens
taxonomy: Oligoneuron × lutescens is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is a cross between Oligoneuron album and Oligoneuron rigidum, two native North American species. As a hybrid, its classification can be a bit complex, with some sources considering it a distinct species and others treating it as a subspecies of one of its parent species. The taxonomy of Oligoneuron × lutescens is still evolving and subject to ongoing research and debate. Further study is needed to fully understand its classification and relationships within the sunflower family.

These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids present in the plant, such as quercetin and kaempferol, are known for their ability to scavenge free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage. Traditionally, the plant's roots were used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The leaves are said to be used to treat skin conditions and wounds. intro: Oligoneuron × lutescens, also known as Golden Ironweed, is a rare and unique plant species that is a hybrid of the American Ironweed (Oligoneuron album) and the Golden Ironweed (Oligoneuron rigidum). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Golden Ironweed include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
